pages.text:
xvii
Acknowledgments
This seventh edition of Braddom’s Physical Medicine and Rehabili-
tation has been made possible by the efforts of more than 100 
authors—physiatrists and other rehabilitation professionals from 
a wide range of practice settings, backgrounds, and specialty areas 
who have given their time, effort, and knowledge because of their 
commitment and dedication to the field and to the individuals 
with disability who they continually strive to partner with to en -
hance their lives. These colleagues and friends have my deepest 
gratitude and respect for their contributions. The individuals who 
have helped, guided, persuaded, cajoled, and at times “strongly 
encouraged” all of these brilliant authors are the six associate edi-
tors—Karen Kowalske, Michelle Miller, Blessen Eapen, Jeffery 
Johns, Amy Bowles, and Henry Lew—who truly have been the 
force driving the process forward and getting the very best for 
each of the chapters and topic areas. I am thankful for their dili -
gence, oversight, and persistence. The editorial team at Elsevier, 
led tirelessly by our Content Strategist, Mary Hegeler; Senior 
Content Development Specialist, Jennifer Shreiner; and Health 
Content Book Specialist, Kamatchi Madhavan,  have been the 
consummate professionals and have again proven themselves to be 
the best of the best.
Finally, a special thanks goes out to the mentors, professors, 
teachers, and practitioners who have helped to educate, shape, 
and train all of the individuals who contributed to this founda-
tional textbook, for it is through their efforts over the past several 
decades that there exist so many skilled and dedicated profession-
als who could bring this book together. Just as with the best 
physical and rehabilitation medicine practices across the globe, it 
takes a dedicated team of professionals working in harmony to 
achieve the best outcomes. This textbook is what happens when 
those people work together for the advancement of the field—a 
great outcome. My warmest thanks.
